evals.prompt: Write a blog post for our client 1906 Newhighs (cannabis brand) targeting the keyword 'best cannabis for focus'. Their existing content sits at 1906newhighs.com. Aim for AI Overview citation within 6 weeks.
evals.expected_output: A complete Mentionstack delivery: full brief presented first (hypothesis, target keywords w/ DataForSEO volume, target LLMs, expected outcome, outline, outbound sources, interlinks), approval gate held before drafting. Draft includes empathetic 2-sentence opener, 1500-2500 words, 1st/3rd person mix, live-search cited sources, JSON-LD Article schema, blacklist passes.
expectations: Brief is presented BEFORE any drafting and explicitly waits for approval
Brief includes hypothesis, target keywords with DataForSEO search volume, target LLMs, expected outcome, outline, outbound source candidates, internal interlinks
Research script (scripts/research.py) is invoked for the keyword
Cannabis-specific compliance considerations surfaced (regulated claims, do-not-mention list)
First two sentences are empathetic and relatable, not throat-clearing
No em dashes anywhere in the draft
blacklist_check.py is invoked and passes before delivery
Output includes Title tag under 60 chars, meta description 120-160 chars, URL slug, JSON-LD Article schema, cited sources block with Title/Publisher/Date/URL, interlink plan, publishing checklist

evals.prompt: Improve this landing page for our client Latico Leathers. Target keyword: 'handmade leather bags'. Current page is thin: 'We make leather bags. Browse our collection.' Goal is to compete in Google AI Overview for the keyword.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the existing page as thin content, presents a Mentionstack brief explaining the gap, gets approval, then drafts a rewrite that includes craftsmanship E-E-A-T signals, comparison to mass-produced alternatives, social proof, and AI-Overview-friendly structure (FAQ, summarized core claims early). Passes blacklist.
expectations: Acknowledges the existing page is thin and explains specifically why it cannot rank or be cited
Brief is presented before any rewriting and waits for approval
Brief target LLMs explicitly include Google AI Overview
Outline has FAQ section to maximize AI Overview citation surface
Rewrite includes E-E-A-T signals (craftsmanship details, materials sourcing, years of experience, specific techniques)
Internal interlinks reference Latico's other pages (collections, about, care guide)
Final output is meaningfully longer and more specific than the original
blacklist_check.py passes — no banned AI-tell words

evals.prompt: Heatmap.com client wants a comparison post: 'click maps vs heatmaps'. We want to push toward heatmaps as the higher-value tool. Heatmap's CTA is a free trial signup.
evals.expected_output: A comparison-format brief with both options presented fairly but data-led toward heatmaps. Approval gate. Draft includes a comparison table, fair pros/cons for both, data-driven argument for heatmaps in specific scenarios, conversion-oriented CTA. Targets commercial investigation intent.
expectations: Brief explicitly addresses commercial investigation intent
Outline includes a comparison structure (table or side-by-side sections)
Both click maps and heatmaps presented fairly — not a one-sided pitch
Heatmap's positioning is supported by specific data points (use cases, conversion lift, depth of insight)
CTA flows naturally from the content toward Heatmap's free trial
Internal interlinks point to Heatmap's other pages (case studies, pricing, docs)
First two sentences are empathetic — drop the reader into a CRO problem they recognize
blacklist_check.py passes
